milestone_brief: Lift CONTEXT.md §9 non-goal "No state persistence" for V1 scope only: save+restore window geometry (size+position), dock layout, last-used variety, and last-used subtype across launches via QSettings. Per-subtype slider values, surface/bg colors, theme preference, display toggles, camera pose, and clip state are EXPLICITLY OUT-OF-SCOPE (V2/V3 follow-ons). V0 → V1 jump only. Implementation: QMainWindow.saveGeometry()/saveState() into a QSettings("AVC", "algebraic-variety-cross-section") store at closeEvent; restoreGeometry/restoreState in __init__ AFTER widget construction (Qt convention — must restore after addDockWidget/setStatusBar/etc.). For last variety+subtype: on _on_variety_changed and _on_subtype_changed, write settings.setValue("LastSession/variety", name) + setValue("LastSession/subtype", name); on __init__ end (after panels built), read both and call self.variety_combo.setCurrentText(saved_variety) if saved_variety in VARIETIES. The setCurrentText fires _on_variety_changed which builds the subtype list; then setCurrentText on subtype_combo restores the subtype. AI-9: closeEvent is the safest persist point (deterministic, no signal storms). AI-2: tests are pure source-text greps asserting QSettings is used, the org+app name match, and the key names match the documented schema — no QApplication construction. AI-15: zero math claim involved. Cross-platform note: QSettings backing store is INI on Linux, plist on macOS, registry on Windows — user-invisible.
research_synthesis: V1 scope only: QApplication.setOrganizationName("AVC") + setApplicationName("AlgebraicVarietyCrossSection") in main() before QApplication(sys.argv) construction; QSettings() no-arg form everywhere else. Key schema: Window/geometry, Window/state, Window/schema_version=1, LastSession/variety, LastSession/subtype. New _save_settings() + _restore_settings() methods on MainWindow. Save: closeEvent FIRST step (before signal disconnect + waitForDone). Live write-back: setValue in _on_variety_changed and _on_subtype_changed for crash safety. Restore: end of __init__ AFTER _setup_shortcuts(). Schema-version guard for graceful V0 fallback. 8 source-grep tests (AI-2/AI-3 compliant). AI-9 verified: restore-time setCurrentText fires existing handlers, write-back is synchronous (no processEvents).
